heading: Pyth-2 bulls diagram layer stabilized as a cyclic table, nested radii model, and cone-top-view analogy
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, bulls, diagrams, ellipse]
body: Built the bulls diagram layer immediately after the stabilized BULLS program lane. `pyth2_bulls_diagrams.py` now turns the page-121 to page-122 prose into reusable artifacts: Figure 26 becomes a quantified cyclic transition table with `Y1 = 891` as the repeated additive constant and kept fractions `5/6`, `9/20`, and `13/42`; Figure 27 becomes a quantified nested-radii model with inner circle radius `891`, intermediate ellipse radii landing at `W1=2226`, `X1=1602`, and `Z1=1580`, and outer boundary radii `2493`, `2471`, and `3117`; Figure 28 is stabilized as a qualitative cone-top-view analogy preserving the same three-layer nesting while replacing the outer ellipse with an outer circle/boundary. The square-number remarks and the exact-focus claim are deliberately not promoted; both remain isolated as source-layer ambiguities in `pyth2_bulls_diagram_lane.json`. This keeps the validated lane on diagram mechanics derived directly from the already-stable bulls equations and solution family.
